summary: Unable to insert document in mongoDB from PHP
description: I want to install php driver. I run the command "composer require "mongodb/mongodb=^1.0.0" with phpDriver "php_mongodb-1.1.8-5.6-ts-vc11-x64" from "http://pecl.php.net/package/mongodb" and add extension in php.ini in php5.6 as well as apache's php.ini. By calling phpinfo(), it is showing installed mongoDriver. But when I call it by  "new MongoDB();" it gives the error "class not found" I tried with "new MongoDB\Client("mongodb://localhost:27017");" but on calling the insert function for adding document. it gives the error that inset function does not exist. Any way of going out?

summary: Do not use system crypto policy for OpenSSL on Windows
description: {{MONGOC_ENABLE_CRYPTO_SYSTEM_PROFILE}} should not be used for our Windows builds. The functionality is primarily intended for Linux distributions with custom profiles (e.g. Fedora). [d227207|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/d227207f970e590c3149979a675b17a0362145fa] for PHPC-703 incorrectly assumed that libmongoc was enabling it by default on Windows.

summary: Do not allow mixing bundled and system libbson and libmongoc libraries
description:
Quoting my comment from PHPC-856:

bq. Also, I would introduce some logic to not allow mixing bundled and system libraries. If libbson is bundled, only the PHP extension is going to use that code, while the libmongoc system library will use its own version of libbson. That could lead to odd behavior down the line.

summary: WriteResult debug handler should return objects
description: Currently, WriteResult's debug handler flattens its internal objects (e.g. upserted IDs, write errors) to arrays. We should expose them as objects, which will allow us to re-use code from the getters and also make {{__debugInfo()}} more usable when we implement PHPC-470.

summary: Assertion failure in stream_not_found() after failed getmore operation
description:
PHPC has two tests for throwing an exception on a failed getmore. These tests spin up their own server with mongo-orchestration and configure the failReceivedGetmore fail point.

After upgrading from 1.5.0-rc3 to 1.5.0-rc4, both of these tests trigger an assert failure in libmongoc:

{noformat}
phpc/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-cluster.c:1470 stream_not_found(): precondition failed: error
Aborted (core dumped)

Termsig=6
{noformat}

Based on the libmongoc trace log, this assert failure happens when the {{mongoc_client_t}} is destroyed during process shutdown. Our trace logs end with:

{noformat}
OK: Got MongoDB\Driver\Exception\ConnectionException
===DONE===

summary: Alphabetize Regex flags when instantiating from BSON
description: PHPC-829 addressed alphabetizing Regex flags when constructing a new object or instantiating from a serialized value, but also need to alphabetize flags when instantiating from raw BSON data.

summary: BSON array keys should be disregarded during visitation
description: BSON corpus spec tests for ["Single Element Array with index set incorrectly"|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/bson-corpus/tests/array.json] currently fail because we always use {{add_assoc()}} when appending elements of a BSON array to a PHP array. If we are visiting a BSON array, each element's key should be disregarded and we should use {{add_next_index()}} instead.

summary: Public property with zero-length name ignored during BSON encoding
description:
BSON corpus spec tests for ["Empty-string key subdoc"|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/bson-corpus/tests/document.json] currently fail because [is_public_property()|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.2/src/bson.c#L1115] returns false for an property whose name is an empty string.

This is an edge case, but such a property name could be created by casting {{['' => 1]}} to an object (i.e. stdClass), which is exactly the code path encountered by the spec test when the BSON data is round-tripped through PHP.

summary: Always recalculate field name length when encoding BSON
description: On PHP 5.x, {{phongo_zval_to_bson()}} uses the length provided by PHP's HashTable API when appending a BSON field. If the field contains multiple null bytes, the driver might append those null bytes to the BSON buffer, thereby shifting the remainder of the document's data.

summary: BSON encoding should throw if PHP keys contain null bytes
description:
When encoding arrays, we should not allow null bytes to appear anywhere in a key string.

When encoding objects, field names with a leading null byte are ignored, since the fields originate from an object's property hash table and PHP uses leading null bytes to denote protected and private properties. By design, the extension ignores protected and private properties.

summary: php_json_serializable_ce missing in PHP versions before 5.5.10 and 5.4.26
description: As [~remi] noted in [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#475|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/475#issuecomment-268556552], the {{php_json_serializable_ce}} symbol was only introduced in PHP 5.6.0, 5.5.10, and 5.4.26 by [this commit|https://github.com/php/php-src/commit/786234d351c40a9b4808d1dd13d2f91f2f5314be]. Earlier versions will need to dynamically fetch the class symbol at runtime.

summary: php_date_get_immutable_ce missing in PHP versions before 5.6.8 and 5.5.24
description: {{php_date_get_immutable_ce()}} was first exported in [this commit|https://github.com/php/php-src/commit/2c117a40a7e7241f911db662b5401ebe2bc3c386].

summary: Windows CA stores should be opened with read-only flag
description: See CDRIVER-1964 for more details. This is a tracking ticket for bumping our libmongoc submodule once the issue is resolved upstream.

summary: BSON get_properties handlers leak during gc_possible_root() checks
readConcern option should not be included in getMore commands
description: See CDRIVER-2003 for more details. This is a tracking ticket for bumping our libmongoc submodule once the issue is resolved upstream.

summary: Child process should not re-use mongoc_client_t objects from parent
description: The hash for persisted mongoc_client_t objects should include the current process ID, which would prevent children from potentially reusing objects created by a parent process.

summary: Cursor::setTypeMap() may unnecessarily convert first BSON document
description: The fix for PHPC-849 always applies the new type map to the cursor's current element by calling [{{phongo_bson_to_zval_ex()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.5/src/MongoDB/Cursor.c#L234] if a current element exists. Since [{{phongo_advance_cursor_and_check_for_error()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.5/php_phongo.c#L556] advances to the cursor's first element to check for an error before {{MongoDB\Driver\Cursor}} is even initialized, any cursor with at least one document in its result set will always have a current element (even before iteration begins). Since the PHP library is prone to calling {{setTypeMap()}} on most cursors it creates, this can lead to redundant BSON conversion as described in [this mongodb-user post|https://groups.google.com/d/msg/mongodb-user/QB44qadSnL4/feF76C3ADgAJ].

summary: Use zend_string_release() to free class names
description:
Generally, the extension uses {{zend_string_release()}} to free {{zend_string}} objects after use, which only frees the string's memory if its reference count dips below 1.

That said, a few places are still using {{zend_string_free()}}, which always asserts that the reference count is zero before freeing the string's memory. Two such occurrences are when freeing the {{zend_string}} created to fetch a {{zend_class_entry}} for a [type map class|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.5/src/bson.c#L1656] and ["__pclass" field|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.5/src/bson.c#L246]. This is problematic if a custom autoloader adds a reference to the class name, which is exactly what [Composer does to cache missing classes|https://github.com/composer/composer/blob/1.3.2/src/Composer/Autoload/ClassLoader.php#L362].

Whenever possible, we should use {{zend_string_release()}} to free {{zend_string}} objects.

summary: Distribute pharext release archive
description:
https://pharext.org is a standalone extension installer where the end-user doesn't need any additional installer (such as pecl, composer, or whatnot).

This would allow us to distribute, if needed, two seperate packages (one for PHP5 and one for PHP7).

currently we upload the vanilla package.tar.gz to GitHub, would be nice to upload this one.

Should be easy to auto-generate using our 'make release' target.

summary: Child processes should not destroy clients created by parent processes
description: As demonstrated in [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#527|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/527], child processes currently destroy all {{mongoc_client_t}} objects in the persistent HashTable during MSHUTDOWN. Ideally, each process should be responsible for closing only clients that it created.

summary: False slaveOk URI option should be ignored
description:
The current logic in [{{php_phongo_apply_rp_options_to_uri()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.3/php_phongo.c#L1076] always assigns a "secondaryPreferred" read preference mode if the "slaveOk" option is found. It should check that the option is also true.

Based on the logic in libmongoc's URI parser, a false value for "slaveOk" should have no affect on the read preference.

summary: sasl authenticate every request whether the connection is open or not
description:
mongodb extension use persist connection between php and mongodb. But I have found that mongodb extension triggers sasl authenticate every reqeust under php fpm mode, whether the connection is open or not.
I have done the stress-test, and the qps grew three times when I closed the sasl auth. CPU is the problem.
How do solve it ? I want to keep the auth work.

summary: localThresholdMS should default to 15 milliseconds
description: Per CDRIVER-2057, libmongoc incorrectly defaults localThresholdMS to 15000 (i.e. 15 seconds) instead of 15 milliseconds. While that will eventually be fixed upstream, we should be able to correct this in the PHP driver by assigning a default as we construct a {{mongoc_uri_t}}.

summary: Possible mongoc_client_t use-after-free with shutdown functions

My initial analysis:

{quote}
Also of note: before I noticed {{mongoc_cluster_fetch_stream_pooled()}} in the crash backtrace, we originally suspected this was a recurrence of PHPC-671 (fixed in 1.1.7), which entailed a crash due to PHP 5.x freeing the MongoDB\Driver\Manager object before open MongoDB\Driver\Cursor objects. That resulted in the Manager also destroying libmongoc's client object (and all streams) and causing a use-after-free when the Cursor wanted to free itself and send a kill command over the stream it was last associated with.

That said, {{mongoc_cluster_fetch_stream_pooled()}} is really the red flag here, as there's no reason that libmongoc should not be in single_threaded mode. See the [{{_mongoc_cluster_stream_for_server_description}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.3.5/src/mongoc/mongoc-cluster.c#L1344] for the relevant logic in libmongoc (one level deep in the backtrace).
{quote}

Most likely, the {{MongoDB\Driver\Manager}} (and its {{mongoc_client_t}}) was again freed before the non-exhausted {{MongoDB\Driver\Cursor}}. This resulted in {{topology->single_threaded}} being zeroed in memory (responsible for libmongoc attempting to fetch a stream from a pool). Somehow, the {{mongoc_cursor_t}} still had a reference to this memory when it attempted to kill itself.

Since [~jwage] was using [mongo-php-adapter|https://github.com/alcaeus/mongo-php-adapter], this issue may be a duplicate of PHPC-732 and caused entirely by the use of generators. We'll need to confirm that.

summary: Possible mongoc_client_t use-after-free with generators
php_phongo_pclient_t is leaked during MSHUTDOWN
description:
PHPC-912 introduced a {{php_phongo_pclient_t}} struct to encapsulate both the {{mongoc_client_t}} and PID, which is persisted across requests. When the persistent hash's destroy function was changed to only free {{mongoc_client_t}} if the PID matched the current process, we neglected to add a free on the {{php_phongo_pclient_t}} wrapper, which should always happen.

This is not a major issue, since the memory is leaked during MSHUTDOWN.

Note: this was discovered while checking for outstanding PHP 7 memory leaks for PHPC-500.

summary: Allow compiling without SSL
Do not throw exceptions for critical and error levels in libmongoc log callback
description:
Currently, {{php_phongo_log()}} throws exceptions for "error" and "critical' log levels and logs to the configured debug stream (e.g. stderr, output file) for all other levels. This is inconsistent for a few reasons:

 * {{mongodb.debug}} is only [documented|http://php.net/manual/en/mongodb.configuration.php#ini.mongodb.debug] as adding background log functionality, while exceptions may disrupt application flow.
 * These exceptions are subject to change by internal changes in libmongoc and do not depend on the PHP extension. As such, new exceptions might show up after upgrading libmongoc if the driver is configured to use shared libraries.
 * We generally check for libmongoc errors already and throw our own exceptions. In these cases, an exception resulting from a libmongoc error may end up as a [previous exception|http://php.net/manual/en/exception.getprevious.php] when we throw our own.
 * {{php_phongo_log()}} calls {{phongo_throw_exception()}}, which calls {{phongo_exception_from_phongo_domain()}}, which calls {{MONGOC_ERROR()}} if the domain is unknown. This is a possible cycle, although it is never triggered since {{phongo_throw_exception()}} always specifies a valid domain. Nevertheless, it would be nice to clean this up.

summary: Unnecessary zend_string_release() in phongo_zval_to_bson() for PHP 7
description: A redundant [{{zend_string_release()}} call|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/504/files#diff-f7d29a680148f52d6601f59ed787f577R1391] introduced in PHPC-884 causes {{bson-fromPHP-006.phpt}} ("BSON\fromPHP(): PHP documents with null bytes in field name") to leak. It appears that the offending line was incorrectly preserved from the previous code, which [always added a reference|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/504/files#diff-f7d29a680148f52d6601f59ed787f577L1429] to existing property names.

summary: Resolve PHP 7 memory leaks
description: The [rebased pull request|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/175] for PHP 7 still has several memory leaks that need to be investigated. Those appear to be the main causes of our test failures.

summary: Using a projection with an empty field name causes a crash when destroying cursor
description: Tracking ticket for CDRIVER-2072

summary: localThresholdMS of zero is not supported
description: This is a tracking ticket for CDRIVER-2056. Currently, libmongoc considers zero to be unset and instead uses the default value for localThresholdMS. Due to a separate bug in CDRIVER-2057, that default is also incorrect (15 seconds instead of 15 milliseconds); however, we can address the incorrect default with PHPC-917 in advance of a libmongoc fix.

summary: Clang errors on unknown options (-Wno-unused-but-set-variable)
description:
trying to build the driver with clang on osx errors on unrecognized warning flag {{-Wno-unused-but-set-variable}}

OUTPUT:

{noformat}
error: unknown warning option '-Wno-unused-but-set-variable'; did you mean
      '-Wno-unused-const-variable'? [-Werror,-Wunknown-warning-option]
make: *** [src/MongoDB/ReadConcern.lo] Error 1
error: unknown warning option '-Wno-unused-but-set-variable'; did you mean
      '-Wno-unused-const-variable'? [-Werror,-Wunknown-warning-option]
error: unknown warning option '-Wno-unused-but-set-variable'; did you mean
      '-Wno-unused-const-variable'? [-Werror,-Wunknown-warning-option]
make: *** [src/MongoDB/Query.lo] Error 1
error: unknown warning option '-Wno-unused-but-set-variable'; did you mean
      '-Wno-unused-const-variable'? [-Werror,-Wunknown-warning-option]
make: *** [src/MongoDB/Manager.lo] Error 1
make: *** [src/MongoDB/CursorId.lo] Error 1
error: unknown warning option '-Wno-unused-but-set-variable'; did you mean
      '-Wno-unused-const-variable'? [-Werror,-Wunknown-warning-option]
make: *** [src/MongoDB/ReadPreference.lo] Error 1
{noformat}

summary: Validate filter and options in Query constructor
description: While the crash in PHPC-920 was ultimately the result of a libmongoc bug (CDRIVER-2072), we could have avoided it by validating the Query's filter and options arguments at construct time and throwing an InvalidArgumentException.

summary: Define PHP_MONGODB_VERSION constant for Pickle compatibility
description: The {{PHP_EXTNAME_VERSION}} convention appears to date back to https://github.com/php/php-src/commit/dcfd753104085d8337b8a5f4a21ce3b629ad4651 and Pickle happens to [require this constant|https://github.com/FriendsOfPHP/pickle/blob/b4ff87802c8c7823f3fb08b4f14dfb0b5492e025/src/Package/Util/Header/Version.php#L90] when installing a package.

summary: php_phongo_free_ssl_opt() attempts to free interned strings
Use mongoc_uri_new_with_error() to improve URI error reporting
description: CDRIVER-782 introduced {{mongoc_uri_new_with_error()}}, which we can utilize to improve error reporting for URI failures.

summary: Driver should validate BSON documents before insert and update
MONGOC_ERROR_COMMAND_INVALID_ARG should yield InvalidArgumentException
description:
Currently, {{MONGOC_ERROR_COMMAND_INVALID_ARG}} error codes yield RuntimeExceptions. Since libmongoc uses this code for argument validation errors in its own API  (before commands would be sent to the server), InvalidArgumentException would be more fitting. Typically, RuntimeExceptions indicate errors originating from the server.

This is relevant to PHPC-712 and CDRIVER-2018.

summary: ReadPreference constructor should accept strings
description: The ReadPreference constructor should accept string values consistent with the [readPreference URI option|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/#urioption.readPreference]. To preserve BC, we should still accept the integer constants.

summary: BSON encoding should throw on circular references
Memory leak if Serializable::bsonSerialize() returns keys with null bytes
description: This leak was introduced in [84dbfe0|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/84dbfe07f7afb2c2ee3ae3fbceeddad0796ad1df] (PHPC-891). When throwing an exception for null bytes in public property names, we neglect to free {{obj_data}} if we were currently iterating through the hash of {{bsonSerialize()}}'s return value.

summary: CursorId is a signed 64-bit integer
description: CursorId currently uses a {{uint64_t}} for storing the ID (and rendering it via {{CursorId::__toString()}}); however, the value should be a signed integer.

summary: Cursor::setTypeMap() and toPHP() should return early if type map is invalid
description: {{Cursor::setTypeMap()}} and {{MongoDB\BSON\toPHP()}} currently proceed with BSON conversion even if {{php_phongo_bson_typemap_to_state()}} throws an exception. Ideally, they should check if {{php_phongo_bson_typemap_to_state()}} fails and return early.

summary: php mongodb connect 'SCRAM Failure: invalid salt length of 0 in sasl step2' exception
Throw exception for invalid BulkWrite::update() newObj argument
description: If the newObj argument contains a mix of atomic modifiers and field names at its top level, we should raise an exception. PHPLIB already checks for this for its CRUD methods, but the driver is prone to doing only a cursory examination so it can delegate to libmongoc's update or replace functions. Currently, those functions have no way to provide a meaningful error that we can turn into an exception (see: CDRIVER-1111), so we should more carefully examining the newObj to ensure it isn't ambiguous.

summary: executeBulkWrite() should throw InvalidArgumentException for empty BulkWrite
description: {{phongo_execute_write()}} currently relies on {{mongoc_bulk_operation_execute()}} to check for an empty BulkWrite object, which means we throw a BulkWriteException (with an empty WriteResult). Since this is just argument validation (it happens before any network IO), we should throw an InvalidArgumentException.

summary: Check for phongo_bson_to_zval_ex() failures in Server methods
description: On the off chance that {{phongo_bson_to_zval_ex()}} errors out on isMaster decoding, we should return before assigning the zval to the return value.
